posts.note: Instagram beautiful carousel - What is it, really? (education, policy-safe)
posts.onImageText: Research compounds 01. What is it, really? A 60-second primer, minus the hype. Simple version: a short chain of amino acids. Short chain - a signal. Long chain - a protein. Long chains are proteins. The short ones are different and your body already makes thousands of them as signaling molecules. Why researchers care: they're precise signals. It tells a cell what to do and only that. That specificity is why they're studied across recovery, metabolism, skin and longevity. The Indexa Standard: every compound ships with a third-party COA. Verify the batch before you trust it. Read the full primer, link in bio. For research and educational use only. Not medical advice.
posts.caption:
What is it, really?

No hype - here's the actual definition. It's a short chain of amino acids: the same building blocks your body already uses to send signals between cells.

Long chains are proteins. The short ones are different - precise signals that tell a cell what to do, and only that. That's why they're studied across recovery, metabolism, skin and longevity.

One rule before you trust any source: read the third-party COA. Every Indexa compound is testable by batch.

Save this for the next time the algorithm won't let someone say the word. Full primer in bio.

-
For research & educational use only. Not medical advice.
